channel.title: Otthon
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian
channel.dc:creator: Karl Sayle

item.title: About
item.description:
The youngest of three children, Ilkka had a brother and a sister with his brother being the eldest. Both his brother and sister were trained from a young age to be excellent fighters, well versed in both combat and diplomacy.
Ilkka did not take well to combat training, certainly not to the level of his brother and sister preferring the book to the sword.
Ilkka’s uncle is king of Korva. Before being cast from the family and the city, Ilkka was 7th in line to the throne behind his:


Aunt ?
Cousin (F)
Cousin (F)
Father ?
Mother ?
Brother ?
Sister ?


Körvá
item.dc:creator: Karl Sayle

item.title: Location
item.description:
Took Ilkka in and gave him a job as an assistant

Kőris
item.dc:creator: Karl Sayle

item.title: Vellamo, father
item.description: Father to Vellamo, Ilkka
item.dc:creator: Karl Sayle

item.title: Vellamo, Géna
item.description:
Mother to Vellamo, Ilkka

Unbeknown to Ilkka and anyone else, including his father, NAME? was a sorceress. Despite her powers, she managed to keep them hidden from everyone.
item.dc:creator: Karl Sayle

item.title: Race/Class
item.description:
Human Warlock

Körvá

Vellamo


Coming from a royal background, Ilkka had a privileged upbringing. However, his people shunned magic of all kinds favouring strength, hard work and traditional ways over the arcane.
Ilkka has been a keen student all of his life with a keen interest in the history of the wider world and secretly, magic. He stumbled upon numerous legends of powerful, oftentimes evil, wizards and unfathomable creatures that rivalled the power of the gods. This was how he found The Great Old One - now his patron after unknowingly making a pact as a young adolescent. This was the birth of Ilkka Vellamo, the Warlock.
Ilkka’s transition to becoming a Warlock was not an overnight event but a slow transition where his powers presented themselves often by accident. Through his fervent studying, he learned of his inadvertent pact and his powers. Although, his knowledge of The Great Old One was very small and spurred an obsession to find out more of this unknown being. The more he learned, the more his powers grew. This was not well received by his family or his people. Whilst his father tried to quash any calls for him to be exiled from the city, a terrible accident resulted in Ilkka banishing his mother to another, unknown realm. This was the last straw, Ilkka was immediately exiled from the city and disowned by his family. He was only 15 years of age. The guilt and sadness that followed plunged Ilkka into years of depression pushing him to the brink of starvation and worse.
After being taken in by a librarian in Kőris a number of years later, he rediscovered his love of books and for research. This reignited his desire to discover more about The Great Old One and through him, find his mother.
Finding his mother and details of his patron, The Great Old One, have been his primary driving force behind becoming an adventurer at the young age of 21. 


The Great Old One
All that is known of Ilkka’s patron is that he/she is some form of unfathomable beast with an understanding of the world and universe rivalled by none.
Ilkkas relationship with his patron borders on an obsession; the more he discovers about his patron, the more his desire grows to ascend to his/her level of knowledge and understanding of the universe. Ilkka believes that his devotion to his patron will lead him to find a way to bring his mother back.
Ilkka is unaware of what his patron expects in return for the powers granted to him - so far, his dedication appears to be enough.
Ilkka receives messages from his patron in the most random of places; books, written in the clouds and in the bark of a tree. These messages are only visible to Ilkka. However, his patron mostly communicates via Ilkkas dreams. These dreams take the form of uncomfortable, almost nightmarish dreams often etched with the guilt of his past.
item.dc:creator: Karl Sayle

item.title: Who
item.description:
Vellamo, Géna
Vellamo, Ilkka


Whilst still coming to terms and trying to understand his growing powers Ilkka inadvertently banished his mother to an unknown realm during a heated argument.

13 Március 1328

Unknown
item.dc:creator: Karl Sayle

item.title: Name
item.description:
Source: The Circle City
Common: Körvá
Elven:
Dwarven:
Orcish:

Moras

Large











City: also known as ‘The Circle City’ due to its impressive circular outer wall

Size: Large city

Region: Moras

The River Kor winds its way through the city from the North East to the Southern border

Even though magic was forbidden and looked down upon as cheap parlour tricks and a way of cheating a good day's work, the people of ? still knew the value of knowledge, and the value of understanding the magical races (‘Know your foe’) and so had a vast resource of books and scripture curated by their most respected sages over the period of the city's history. 

Despite his people's outlook on magic, the restricted area had a sizable collection of books on the arcane. and the occult which was restricted to all but the highest ranking military officials, sages, politicians and members of the royal family.

With Ilkka being a member of the royal family, albeit a fairly minor royal, he was privileged enough to be allowed access to the restricted areas of the city library.

The people of ? pride themselves on being a democratic and fair society where men and women are seen as equals. They have a strong military force that protects their people and interests both domestically and abroad governed primarily by parliament but also answers to the royal family as has been the city's tradition for centuries.

They have a strong work ethic - a hard day's work is a hard day's work regardless of whether you are a knight or a farmer, they are all equally respected.
item.dc:creator: Karl Sayle
